page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 192 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in multi-core and specialized execution workloads. The instance achieves a strong rating in PassMark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Memory bandwidth for large, potentially uncached blocks is also strong, reaching 259.28 GB/sec for read operations and 114.19 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core Geekbench scores and ray tracing benchmarks fall into the top 25% tier. For language model workloads, prompt processing speeds are strong for small and medium models, though text generation for larger models remains in the average tier. Single-core performance across stress-ng and Geekbench tests is rated as average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-standard-48 offers a balanced resource density suitable for standard enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy operations. The instance is positioned for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel x86_64 architecture with 24 physical cores and hyper-threading. It is configured with 192 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core performance, particularly in PassMark CPU tests, database operations, and large-block memory bandwidth, while single-core metrics remain average. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for standard enterprise workloads by avoiding the premium pricing of specialized compute or memory tiers. The instance is suited for database operations, static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server with 48 Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring forty-eight dedicated Intel vCPUs and strong multi-core performance for enterprise database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 192 GB of system memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Strong multi-core performance in database operations and web serving
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for multi-threaded compilation and medium-scale LLM prompt processing
